1. src/store/rewardProgram/api/orderDetail.api.ts

Summarize:
- The SOLID architecture implementation
- API, Repository, Service, Store layer flow
- Model types and contracts
- Any issues or inconsistencies with the STYLE_GUIDE.md

content.text: Now let me check for the STYLE_GUIDE.md to validate against project standards.

content.content:
1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    12→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    13→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    15→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    16→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    17→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    18→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    19→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    20→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    21→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    22→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Introduction
    27→
    28→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    29→
    30→Key decisions:
    31→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    32→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    33→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    34→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    39→
    40→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    41→
    42→```
    43→screens/Home/
    44→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    45→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    46→└── Models/
    47→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    48→```
    49→
    50→**Models/index.d.ts**
    51→```ts
    52→export interface HomeParams {
    53→  welcome?: string;
    54→}
    55→export interface HomeState {
    56→  greeting: string;
    57→  isLoading: boolean;
    58→}
    59→export interface HomeHandlers {
    60→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    61→}
    62→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    63→```
    64→
    65→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    66→```ts
    67→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from "react";
    68→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from "./Models";
    69→import { useUserStore } from "@/services/user/store"; // Zustand slice example
    70→
    71→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    72→  const name = useUserStore((s) => s.name);
    73→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    74→
    75→  useEffect(() => {
    76→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    77→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    78→  }, []);
    79→
    80→  const greeting = useMemo(
    81→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || "guest"}!`,
    82→    [params?.welcome, name]
    83→  );
    84→
    85→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    86→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    87→  }, []);
    88→
    89→  return { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA };
    90→}
    91→```
    92→
    93→**view.tsx (render)**
    94→```tsx
    95→import React from "react";
    96→import { View } from "react-native";
    97→import { Text, Button } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
    98→import { useHomeScreen } from "./index";
    99→import type { HomeParams } from "./Models";
   100→
   101→type Props = HomeParams;
   102→
   103→export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   104→  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   105→
   106→  if (isLoading) {
   107→    return (
   108→      <View>
   109→        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   110→      </View>
   111→    );
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  return (
   115→    <View>
   116→      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   117→      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   118→    </View>
   119→  );
   120→}
   121→```
   122→
   123→---
   124→
   125→## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern
   126→
   127→```
   128→atomic/atoms/Button/
   129→├── index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   130→├── view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   131→└── Models/
   132→    └── index.d.ts
   133→```
   134→
   135→**Models/index.d.ts**
   136→```ts
   137→export interface ButtonProps {
   138→  text: string;
   139→  disabled?: boolean;
   140→  onPress?: () => void;
   141→}
   142→export interface ButtonState {
   143→  pressed: boolean;
   144→}
   145→export interface ButtonHandlers {
   146→  handlePress: () => void;
   147→}
   148→export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   149→```
   150→
   151→**index.ts (logic hook)**
   152→```ts
   153→import { useCallback, useState } from "react";
   154→import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from "./Models";
   155→
   156→export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   157→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   158→
   159→  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   160→    setPressed(true);
   161→    props.onPress?.();
   162→    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   163→  }, [props.onPress]);
   164→
   165→  return { pressed, handlePress };
   166→}
   167→```
   168→
   169→**view.tsx (render)**
   170→```tsx
   171→import React from "react";
   172→import { Pressable, View } from "react-native";
   173→import { Text } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
   174→import { useButton } from "./index";
   175→import type { ButtonProps } from "./Models";
   176→
   177→export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   178→  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   179→
   180→  return (
   181→    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   182→      <View>
   183→        <Text>{props.text}{pressed ? " •" : ""}</Text>
   184→      </View>
   185→    </Pressable>
   186→  );
   187→}
   188→```
   189→
   190→---
   191→
   192→## Project Organization
   193→
   194→```
   195→src/
   196→├── @types/            # Global TS types
   197→├── assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   198→├── atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   199→│   ├── atoms/
   200→│   ├── molecules/
   201→│   └── organisms/
   202→├── modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   203→├── screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   204→├── services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   205→│   ├── api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   206→│   ├── class/         # Class with logical requests
   207→│   ├── hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   208→│   ├── repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   209→│   ├── services/      # Business orchestration
   210→│   ├── storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   211→│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   212→│   ├── models/        # Domain types & contracts
   213→│   ├── utils/         # Reusable service helps
   214→│   └── index.ts       # Public exports
   215→├── hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   216→├── routes/            # Navigation config
   217→├── settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   218→└── utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   219→```
   220→
   221→> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   222→
   223→---
   224→
   225→## Services Architecture
   226→
   227→**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
   228→
   229→- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
   230→- **class/**: class with logical requests.
   231→- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   232→- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
   233→- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
   234→- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
   235→- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
   236→- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
   237→- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   238→
   239→Principles:
   240→- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   241→- No circular dependencies.
   242→- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## State Management (Zustand-only)
   247→
   248→- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
   249→- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
   250→- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
   251→- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   252→
   253→**Slice example**
   254→```ts
   255→import type { StateCreator } from "zustand";
   256→import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from "../models/store";
   257→
   258→export type AuthSlice = {
   259→    auth: AuthState;
   260→    authActions: AuthActions
   261→};
   262→
   263→const initialAuth = {
   264→    user: null,
   265→    loading: false,
   266→    error: undefined,
   267→}
   268→
   269→export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<AuthSlice, [], [], AuthSlice> = (set) => ({
   270→    auth: initialAuth,
   271→    authActions: {
   272→        setLoading: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, isLoading: v } })),
   273→        setUser: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, user: v } })),
   274→        setError: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, error: v } })),
   275→        reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   276→    }
   277→});
   278→```
   279→
   280→**Root Store**
   281→
   282→- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
   283→
   284→```ts
   285→import { create } from "zustand";
   286→import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from "zustand/middleware";
   287→import { immer } from "zustand/middleware/immer";
   288→
   289→import { createUserSlice, type UserSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user";
   290→import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-yourmf";
   291→
   292→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   293→
   294→/** Add your slice Type */
   295→export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   296→
   297→export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   298→  devtools(
   299→    persist(
   300→      immer((...a) => ({
   301→        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   302→        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   303→      })),
   304→      {
   305→        name: "root-storage",
   306→        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   307→        partialize: (s) => ({
   308→          user: s.user,
   309→          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   310→        }),
   311→        merge: (persisted, current) => ({ ...current, ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>) }),
   312→      }
   313→    ),
   314→    { name: "RootStore" }
   315→  )
   316→);
   317→```
   318→
   319→- For Use on Class.
   320→
   321→```ts
   322→class Auth {
   323→  private readonly setAuth;
   324→
   325→  constructor () {
   326→    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   327→  }
   328→
   329→  function doSomething(data) {
   330→    this.setUser({ ...data });
   331→  }
   332→}
   333→```
   334→
   335→- For Use on Component/Screen.
   336→- UI never imports the store directly — uses selectors
   337→
   338→```ts
   339→const loading = useRootStore((s) => s.user.isLoading);
   340→const token = useRootStore((s) => s.auth.token);
   341→```
   342→
   343→---
   344→
   345→## Routes Naming
   346→
   347→Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:
   348→- ✅ `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)
   349→- ✅ `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
   350→
   351→---
   352→
   353→## Folder Naming Rules
   354→
   355→| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                    |
   356→|-------------------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------|
   357→| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails`  |
   358→| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`    |
   359→
   360→---
   361→
   362→## Code Standards
   363→
   364→**Language**: English only.  
   365→**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
   366→
   367→**Explanatory variables**
   368→```js
   369→const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
   370→const getNumberValidation = (message) => (value) => !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
   371→const isNumber = getNumberValidation("error message");
   372→isNumber(1);
   373→```
   374→
   375→**Semantic constants**
   376→```js
   377→const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
   378→setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
   379→```
   380→
   381→**Library workarounds with link**
   382→```js
   383→// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
   384→// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
   385→event.target.value = "";
   386→```
   387→
   388→**JSDoc**
   389→```js
   390→/**
   391→ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
   392→ * @param {*} eventName
   393→ * @param {*} maxLength
   394→ */
   395→```
   396→
   397→**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**
   398→```ts
   399→useEffect(() => {
   400→  (async () => {
   401→    await CustomMath.randomSum();
   402→    setSum(42);
   403→  })();
   404→}, [
   405→  setSum,
   406→]);
   407→```
   408→
   409→**Extract conditions**
   410→```ts
   411→const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
   412→if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
   413→```
   414→
   415→**Optional chaining**
   416→```ts
   417→const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
   418→```
   419→
   420→**JSX indentation**
   421→```tsx
   422→{
   423→  !isEnd && (
   424→    <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />
   425→  )
   426→}
   427→```
   428→
   429→**Early returns**
   430→```ts
   431→if (!data) return null;
   432→return <View />;
   433→```
   434→
   435→**Nullish coalescing**
   436→```ts
   437→return data ?? 0;
   438→```
   439→---
   440→
   441→## Styles Patterns
   442→
   443→- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.
   444→- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
   445→
   446→---
   447→
   448→## Cache Pattern
   449→
   450→Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   451→
   452→```ts
   453→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   454→
   455→export interface CachedData<T> {
   456→  timestamp: number;
   457→  data: T;
   458→}
   459→
   460→export interface StorageContract<T> {
   461→  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   462→  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   463→  remove(): Promise<void>;
   464→}
   465→
   466→export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   467→  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   468→
   469→  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   470→    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   471→    if (!json) return null;
   472→    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   473→  }
   474→
   475→  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   476→    const payload: CachedData<T> = { timestamp: Date.now(), data };
   477→    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   478→  }
   479→
   480→  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   481→    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   482→  }
   483→}
   484→```
   485→
   486→**Guidelines**
   487→- One storage class per module/feature.
   488→- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   489→- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   490→- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   491→- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
   492→
   493→---
   494→
   495→## Checklist
   496→
   497→- [ ] Screens/Components follow **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` hook + `view.tsx` render)
   498→- [ ] Types live in **Models/**
   499→- [ ] Only **Zustand** for global/shared state under **/services/<domain>/store/**
   500→- [ ] UI never calls API directly (go through **/services**)
   501→- [ ] Code standards and styles patterns applied
   502→- [ ] Cache implemented via **GenericStorage** (when needed)
   503→- [ ] Routes and folder naming conventions followed
   504→
   505→---
   506→
   507→Happy shipping! 🚀

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.text:
## Analysis Report: OrderDetail Domain Architecture

### 1. SOLID Architecture Implementation

The orderDetail domain demonstrates a **well-structured SOLID architecture** with proper separation of concerns:

**Single Responsibility Principle (SRP)**
- **api/orderDetail.api.ts**: HTTP communication only (DTOs, request/response contracts)
- **repository/orderDetail.repository.ts**: Data transformation (DTO → domain model)
- **services/orderDetail.service.ts**: Business orchestration (flows, error handling, state coordination)
- **store/orderDetail.store.ts**: State mutation (Zustand slice, action creators)

**Dependency Inversion**
- Each layer depends on abstractions (interfaces/types) rather than concrete implementations
- Service layer abstracts API calls through the repository pattern
- Store actions are injected via `useRootStore.getState()` (dependency injection)

**Open/Closed Principle**
- New API response formats can be handled by extending repository mappers without modifying service logic
- Status enums are extensible for new states

---

### 2. Layer Flow Architecture

The flow follows the prescribed pattern: **UI → Services → Repository → API**

```
getOrderDetail(payload)
    ↓
[Service: orderDetail.service.ts]
    ├─ Validates payload (transactionId, participantId)
    ├─ Manages loading state via store actions
    ├─ Handles duplicate requests (isDifferentTransaction check)
    ↓
getOrderDetailApi(requestPayload)
    ↓
[API: orderDetail.api.ts]
    ├─ POST to 'fintech/v1/rewardProgram/extract-details'
    ├─ Returns AxiosResponse<OrderDetailApiResponse>
    ↓
getOrderDetailFromResponse(response)
    ↓
[Repository: orderDetail.repository.ts]
    ├─ Validates HTTP status (returnCode === 200 || 0)
    ├─ Transforms DTO → PointsTransaction domain model
    ├─ Maps items/benefits to detail fields
    ↓
[Store: orderDetail.store.ts]
    ├─ setOrderDetail() updates state with transformed data
    ├─ setOrderDetailStatus() manages loading/error states
    ├─ reset() clears state
    ↓
UI reads from store via selectors
```

**Key strengths:**
- Clear responsibility boundaries
- No business logic leakage into UI
- API changes isolated to repository layer
- Easy to mock/test each layer independently

---

### 3. Model Types & Contracts

**API Layer (DTOs)** — `orderDetail.api.ts`
- `OrderDetailItemDto`: Product item (id, unitaryValue, quantity, description)
- `OrderDetailBenefitDto`: Applied benefits (id, type, value, description)
- `MarketplaceDto`: Nested marketplace context (orders, payments, shipping points)
- `OrderDetailApiResponse`: HTTP envelope with data + status codes

**Domain Model** — `orderDetail.model.ts` + `extract.model.ts`
- `OrderDetailState`: State shape (orderDetail?, status, error, transactionId)
- `PointsTransaction`: Rich domain model (id, date, type, icon, details.fields)
- `OrderDetailStatus`: Enum (IDLE, LOADING, SUCCESS, ERROR)

**Transformation Bridge** — `orderDetail.repository.ts`
```typescript
// Input: OrderDetailItemDto[]
items.forEach(item => {
  fields.push({
    label: `Item ${index + 1}`,
    value: `${item.description} - ${item.quantity}x R$ ${item.unitaryValue.toFixed(2)}`
  })
})

// Output: PointsTransaction with computed currencyValue
```

**Critical Design Pattern:** The repository **computes derived data** (totalValue, formatted fields) from DTOs, ensuring the store never contains unprocessed transport data.

---

### 4. Issues & Style Guide Violations

#### 4.1 Import Path Violation
**Location:** `services/orderDetail.service.ts` (line 3)
```typescript
import { useRootStore } from '../..';  // ❌ RELATIVE PATH
```

**Issue:** According to STYLE_GUIDE.md (line 31): *"Store imports **MUST** use `~/store`"*

**Expected:**
```typescript
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
```

---

#### 4.2 Missing Circular Dependency Guard
**Location:** `models/orderDetail.model.ts` (line 1)
```typescript
import { PointsTransaction } from './extract.model';
```

**Location:** `repository/orderDetail.repository.ts` (line 1)
```typescript
import { PointsTransaction } from '../models/extract.model';
```

**Issue:** The orderDetail domain depends on extract.model, but these should be in separate domains if orderDetail is a discrete feature. If intentional code sharing, the imports should document the cross-domain dependency.

**Recommendation:** Verify this is intentional; if yes, document in a `README.md` or add a comment explaining the shared data contract.

---

#### 4.3 Incomplete Error Handling
**Location:** `services/orderDetail.service.ts` (lines 76-78)
```typescript
} catch {
  handleOrderDetailError();  // Generic catch-all, error not logged/tracked
}
```

**Issue:** No error context captured (network failures, parse errors, timeout). This makes debugging production issues difficult.

**Expected pattern from STYLE_GUIDE:**
```typescript
} catch (error) {
  console.error('Failed to fetch order detail:', error);
  handleOrderDetailError();
}
```

---

#### 4.4 Type Safety Gap in Repository
**Location:** `repository/orderDetail.repository.ts` (line 48)
```typescript
export function getOrderDetailFromResponse(
  response: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getOrderDetailApi>>,
): PointsTransaction | null {
```

**Issue:** Complex computed type makes the contract unclear. The function signature doesn't explicitly state it expects `AxiosResponse<OrderDetailApiResponse>`.

**Expected:**
```typescript
import type { AxiosResponse } from 'axios';
import type { OrderDetailApiResponse } from '../api/orderDetail.api';

export function getOrderDetailFromResponse(
  response: AxiosResponse<OrderDetailApiResponse> | undefined,
): PointsTransaction | null {
```

---

#### 4.5 Marketplace Data Propagation Without Validation
**Location:** `repository/orderDetail.repository.ts` (line 44)
```typescript
marketplace: dto.marketplace,  // Passed through without type narrowing
```

**Issue:** `MarketplaceDto` contains nullable fields (`orders?`, `payments?`). The domain model doesn't validate marketplace structure before storing.

**Recommendation:** Add explicit validation or use `as const` type narrowing.

---

#### 4.6 Missing JSDoc Comments
**Location:** All service functions lack JSDoc

**Current:**
```typescript
export async function getOrderDetail(payload: GetOrderDetailPayload): Promise<void> {
```

**Expected per STYLE_GUIDE (lines 388-395):**
```typescript
/**
 * Fetches detailed order information for a specific transaction.
 * Handles participant ID resolution from token and manages request deduplication.
 * @param {GetOrderDetailPayload} payload - transactionId (required), participantId (optional)
 */
export async function getOrderDetail(payload: GetOrderDetailPayload): Promise<void> {
```

---

#### 4.7 Store State Structure Anomaly
**Location:** `store/orderDetail.store.ts` (lines 35-46)
```typescript
setOrderDetail: (orderDetail, transactionId) =>
  set((state) => ({
    orderDetail: {
      ...state.orderDetail,        // ❌ Spreading OrderDetailState
      orderDetail,                 // ❌ Setting nested orderDetail property
      status: OrderDetailStatus.SUCCESS,
      transactionId,
    },
  })),
```

**Issue:** The state structure is confusing. `orderDetail.orderDetail` is a nested property that shadows the slice name.

**Expected structure:**
```typescript
// In store/orderDetail.store.ts
const INITIAL_ORDER_DETAIL_STATE: OrderDetailState = {
  data: undefined,           // Not: orderDetail
  status: OrderDetailStatus.IDLE,
  error: undefined,
  transactionId: undefined,
};

setOrderDetail: (transaction, transactionId) =>
  set((state) => ({
    orderDetail: {
      ...state.orderDetail,
      data: transaction,            // Clear naming
      status: OrderDetailStatus.SUCCESS,
      transactionId,
    },
  })),
```

---

#### 4.8 Utility Import Inconsistency
**Location:** `services/orderDetail.service.ts` (line 8)
```typescript
import { getParticipantIdFromToken } from '../utils';  // ✅ Correct (relative)
```

**Location:** Same file (line 7)
```typescript
import { eventServerResponse } from '~/constants/functions';  // ✅ Correct (alias)
```

**Issue:** Mixed import patterns (relative for local utils, `~/` for SuperApp constants). Per STYLE_GUIDE, maintain consistency: use `~/` for anything outside the domain, relative only within domain.

---

### 5. Summary

| Aspect | Status | Notes |
|--------|--------|-------|
| **SOLID Principles** | ✅ Strong | Clear SRP, DI via store, extensible design |
| **Layer Flow** | ✅ Correct | API → Repository → Service → Store chain |
| **Type Safety** | ⚠️ Partial | Generic computed types, missing validation |
| **Error Handling** | ⚠️ Weak | Silent catch-all, no error context |
| **Store State Shape** | ❌ Confusing | Nested `orderDetail.orderDetail` property |
| **Documentation** | ❌ Missing | No JSDoc on service functions |
| **Style Compliance** | ⚠️ 70% | Relative import path violation (#4.1), mixed patterns |
| **Testing Readiness** | ✅ Good | Layers are mutable/mockable |

**Critical fixes required before merge:**
1. Change store import from relative (`../..`) to `~/store`
2. Clarify orderDetail state nesting (rename `orderDetail` property to `data`)
3. Add error context capture in service catch block
4. Add JSDoc comments to service layer functions
